chapter_context.summary: Jacob arrives in Haran and marries Leah and Rachel after serving Laban.

outline.text: Jacob Arrives in Haran and Meets Rachel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jacob Arrives in Haran and Meets Rachel (29:1-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Genesis.
This section focuses on Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel (29:15-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Genesis.
This section focuses on Leah Bears Sons (29:31-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Genesis.

key_themes.title: Covenant Promises
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promises of land, offspring, and global blessing to Abraham and his seed.

key_themes.title: Providential Guidance
key_themes.description: God working through human events, choices, and difficulties to preserve His chosen line.

devotional.question: What does Genesis 29 teach you about God's faithfulness to His promises despite human failures?
How can you apply the lessons of faith and obedience from this chapter in your life today?

cultural_historical.title: Patriarchal Era
cultural_historical.text: Chapters 12-50 focus on the families of Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph, establishing the roots of the nation of Israel.
